question_text: A student investigates friction by pulling a wooden block across a rough surface with a force meter. The force meter reads 8 N when the block moves at constant velocity. The block has a mass of 2 kg. Calculate the coefficient of friction between the block and the surface. (Take g = 10 m/s²)
explanation: When the block moves at constant velocity, the applied force equals the friction force (8 N). The normal force equals the weight: N = mg = 2 × 10 = 20 N. Coefficient of friction μ = friction force ÷ normal force = 8 ÷ 20 = 0.4. This requires understanding that constant velocity means balanced forces, then using the friction formula.
